task: Initial project scaffold: Full-stack expense tracker with Go backend, React frontend, Turso DB
logic_changed: Established the entire layered architecture: config (custom .env parser, not godotenv) -> database (Turso/libsql with connection pool: 25 open, 5 idle, 5min lifetime) -> repository (SQL via libsql) -> service (business logic) -> handler (HTTP parsing) -> router (Chi). Frontend is a state-based SPA (no React Router) with App.tsx as the top-level state machine. Auth middleware injects userID/role/groupID into request context via custom contextKey types. Database uses UUIDs as TEXT primary keys. Server has graceful shutdown on SIGINT/SIGTERM with 30s timeout. Currency is Thai Baht.
ai_warning: The custom .env parser in config.go only sets env vars if NOT already defined (env vars take precedence). PORT env var takes precedence over SERVER_PORT which defaults to 10000. The database schema uses migrations that ignore errors on ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN (columns may already exist). Nullable fields (status, paidAmount, receiptImage) use sql.Null* types -- never assume they are non-null without checking .Valid.

task: Category management system: DB schema with seed data, CRUD repository, and handler layers
logic_changed: Added categories table with UNIQUE(name, type, group_id, user_id) constraint. Seeded default categories: INCOME (7: Salary, Freelance, Investment, Rental, Gift, Refund, Other) and EXPENSE (11: Food & Dining through Other) using INSERT OR IGNORE for idempotency. Categories can be global (group_id IS NULL AND user_id IS NULL) or group/user-specific. GetAll query uses UNION to merge group-specific + global categories. Created partial unique index for global categories: WHERE group_id IS NULL AND user_id IS NULL. Category migration uses rename-recreate-copy-drop pattern for adding user_id column to existing tables.
ai_warning: The UNIQUE constraint includes group_id and user_id -- different groups can have categories with the same name+type. When deleting a category, always check IsCategoryInUse() first to avoid breaking FK references. The seed runs on every migration but is idempotent. The migration for adding user_id column is complex (rename old table, create new, copy data, drop old) -- do not modify without understanding the full flow.

task: Transaction management: Full CRUD with income/expense type discrimination, batch operations, annual summaries
logic_changed: Type-discriminated transaction system: INCOME transactions have NULL status and paidAmount (strictly enforced in validation); EXPENSE transactions default to PENDING and 0 paidAmount. Business rules: (1) Attaching receipt auto-sets status to PAID and paidAmount to full amount; (2) Setting status to PAID requires a receipt image; (3) When marking PAID without specifying paidAmount, repository defaults to full transaction amount. Batch create/delete use database transactions. Annual summary pre-initializes all 12 months to zero and aggregates by category using 'type|category' composite key. Date range queries use exclusive upper bound (date < nextMonth) to avoid off-by-one.
ai_warning: Critical bug fixed later: Update had duplicate SET clause when status=PAID and receipt were both provided. The guard 'req.Status == nil' before receipt-auto-PAID block prevents this (see BUG_FIXES.md FIX-02). Transaction dates use string format YYYY-MM-DD with regex + time.Parse validation. Month boundary calculation: month 12 wraps to year+1 month 1. Do NOT change the date format without updating both frontend and backend.

task: Authentication system: JWT with password peppering, bcrypt hashing, admin role from env config
logic_changed: Three-layer password security: (1) Client sends plaintext password; (2) Server peppers with SHA256(password:username:pepperSecret) where pepperSecret comes from PEPPER_SECRET env var; (3) Peppered hash is bcrypt'd with DefaultCost. Admin role determined by checking username against comma-separated ADMIN_USERNAMES env var (was hardcoded, fixed in BUG_FIXES.md FIX-04). On registration, a personal group is auto-created ('{username}'s Group') and user added as member. JWT tokens embed sub, username, role, groupId with 7-day expiry. AuthMiddleware extracts claims via comma-ok pattern to prevent panic (fixed from direct type assertion, FIX-01).
ai_warning: JWT_SECRET, PEPPER_SECRET, and ADMIN_USERNAMES are all required -- app calls log.Fatal if missing. The peppering scheme means changing PEPPER_SECRET or a username invalidates ALL existing passwords. GroupRepository.AddMember() enforces 1-user-1-group by DELETing existing memberships first in a transaction. GenerateToken uses context.Background() instead of propagating caller context -- a known code smell. Auth middleware returns plain-text errors while handlers return JSON -- inconsistency noted in AUDIT_REPORT.md.

task: Transaction form: Income/expense toggle, receipt image compression, batch copy from another month
logic_changed: Type-aware form toggling between INCOME and EXPENSE modes, resetting category on switch. INCOME submissions omit status/paidAmount; EXPENSE includes both. Receipt images are client-side compressed via Canvas: max 1000x1000px, JPEG quality 0.7, converted to Base64 data URL. Adding receipt to EXPENSE auto-sets status to PAID and paidAmount to amount. Paid Amount input disabled when status is PENDING. Batch copy via CopyTransactionsModal copies transactions from another month. Form collapses by default on mobile (viewport <= 768px). Error/success states are inline with auto-dismiss.
ai_warning: TransactionFormState is a flat object that doesn't leverage TypeScript discriminated unions -- status and paidAmount exist as strings even for INCOME mode. The compressImage function is a Promise wrapper around FileReader+Image+Canvas; errors in image loading may be hard to debug. Categories come from API but types/transaction.ts also has hardcoded INCOME_CATEGORIES/EXPENSE_CATEGORIES const arrays -- potential drift from DB-seeded defaults.

task: Admin panel and group management: User/group CRUD APIs with multi-user isolation and member operations
logic_changed: Admin routes under /api/admin/* with double middleware: AuthMiddleware (JWT) + AdminOnly (role check). Endpoints: ListUsers, UpdateUserStatus (ACTIVE/INACTIVE toggle), ListGroups, GetGroupMembers, AddMemberToGroup, RemoveMemberFromGroup. RemoveMember implements 4-step transactional flow: Look up username -> DELETE from group_members -> Find personal group by name pattern '{username}'s Group' -> INSERT OR IGNORE back to personal group (ensures user always has one group). UpdateStatus checks RowsAffected and returns error on zero rows (fixed from silent 200, FIX-07).
ai_warning: AdminOnly middleware compares role as string(enum) -- if role values change in models, comparison breaks silently. RemoveMember personal group lookup by name pattern is fragile: group renames break it. User listing for admin excludes password_hash column, which is correct. The 1-user-1-group invariant is enforced at the DB+repo level but UI should also prevent adding a user to multiple groups.

task: Annual dashboard: Year-level summary with recharts-powered monthly and category visualizations
logic_changed: Annual analytics page fetches year-level data via GET /api/transactions/annual?year=Y. Three KPI cards: Total Income, Total Expense, Net Balance. MonthlyMixedChart: recharts BarChart with dual bars (income green, expense red) and line overlay for net balance. CategoryDonutChart: donut chart splitting expense categories with percentage labels. YearPicker persists selected year to localStorage (key: annual_year). All charts use premium dark theme color palette with CSS variables. ResponsiveContainer handles sizing.
ai_warning: Annual summary aggregates all transactions for a year in-memory -- for large datasets this could be a memory concern. CategoryMap uses 'type|category' pipe-delimited composite key -- if pipe appears in a category name, aggregation breaks. Monthly data pre-initializes all 12 months to zero. Charts need a parent with explicit height for ResponsiveContainer to work.

task: Auth context, API client with JWT handling, and centralized transaction management hook
logic_changed: AuthContext: login/logout/isAuthenticated/isAdmin with localStorage persistence (keys: auth_token, auth_user). apiFetch attaches Bearer token from localStorage. On 401 response, dispatches custom DOM event 'auth:expired' instead of calling logout() directly -- this prevents circular re-render loops when a hook fires before auth state is confirmed. App.tsx listens for 'auth:expired' and calls logout(), re-rendering to login without hard reload. useTransactions centralizes all state: month/year filters (persisted to localStorage), CRUD operations, auto-refresh after mutations. useCategories fetches and splits into income/expense lists.
ai_warning: The 401 handling via custom DOM event is deliberate to break circular dependency: apiFetch can't import AuthContext, and dispatching an event avoids hard reloads that cause fetch-on-login-page loops. localStorage for tokens is NOT HttpOnly -- vulnerable to XSS (mitigated by CORS config). useTransactions calls refresh() on every month/year change including initial mount. The auth:expired event is listened to in a useEffect that depends on logout function reference.

task: Chi router initialization: Complete middleware chain, CORS config, all API endpoint wiring
logic_changed: Middleware stack: Logger -> Recoverer -> RequestID -> RealIP -> Heartbeat(/health) -> CORS. CORS allows specific origins (Vercel production URL, localhost:3000, localhost:5173, 127.0.0.1:5173) with AllowCredentials:true -- NO wildcard (fixed from earlier '*'+credentials bug, FIX-03). API routes nested: /auth/* (public), /api/transactions/* (auth-required with all CRUD + batch + annual endpoints), /api/categories/* (auth-required), /api/admin/* (auth + admin). Chi Route() and Group() create scoped middleware. Validation enforces: INCOME must NOT have status/paidAmount; EXPENSE defaults missing status to PENDING; PAID requires paidAmount > 0 (relaxed in update path, FIX-06).
ai_warning: CORS MaxAge is 300 seconds -- browsers cache preflight for 5 minutes, so origin changes may not take effect immediately. Heartbeat middleware hijacks /health at router level before any middleware including auth -- intentional for health checks but means /health bypasses all security. Validation error messages are user-facing: avoid leaking internal state. The writeJSON/writeError helpers are defined in handlers/transaction.go but used by all other handlers -- should be extracted to shared helpers.go.

task: Critical bug fix batch: 10 issues across auth, CORS, validation, repository, and frontend (BUG_FIXES.md)
logic_changed: FIX-01: AuthMiddleware JWT claims extraction switched to comma-ok pattern to prevent panic on malformed tokens. FIX-02: Transaction Update() added guard req.Status == nil before auto-appending status in receipt block to prevent duplicate SET clause. FIX-03: CORS switched from wildcard to explicit origin list with AllowCredentials. FIX-04: Admin role from hardcoded usernames to ADMIN_USERNAMES env var (comma-separated). FIX-05: 401 handling uncommented in apiFetch, dispatches auth:expired event for auto-logout. FIX-06: ValidateUpdateRequest relaxed to allow PAID without explicit paidAmount (repository defaults to full amount). FIX-07: UpdateStatus checks RowsAffected and returns error on zero rows. FIX-08: GetAll categories UNIONs global categories (group_id IS NULL AND user_id IS NULL). FIX-09: CreateUser timestamp unified to single now value. FIX-10: Replaced custom joinStrings with strings.Join.
ai_warning: FIX-01 is critical: NEVER use bare type assertions on JWT claims -- panics kill the handler goroutine (Recoverer catches it but returns 500). FIX-02's guard pattern (checking req.Status == nil for duplicate prevention) is fragile -- if more fields are added to update flow, same class of bug could recur. FIX-06 creates a subtle contract: validation trusts repository to default paidAmount when marking PAID -- changing repository behavior breaks this contract. FIX-08's UNION query arg ordering is critical: groupID prepended via append([]interface{}{groupID}, args...).

task: Mobile UX fix: Add Transaction form responsive behavior on small screens
logic_changed: Transaction form defaults to collapsed state on mobile (viewport <= 768px). Added expand/collapse CSS styles. Form body uses CSS transition for smooth animation. Toggle button and title placed in section header for space efficiency.
ai_warning: Mobile detection uses window.innerWidth in a useEffect that runs once on mount -- does NOT respond to window resize or device rotation. This is intentional to prevent jarring UX mid-interaction, but means initial state may be wrong after resize. The collapsed state hides all form fields; ensure keyboard users can still access the form when expanded.

task: Multi-language (TH/EN i18n) and dark/light theme toggle with persisted user preferences
logic_changed: LanguageContext: stores 'th'|'en' in localStorage, exposes t via useMemo. ThemeContext: stores 'light'|'dark' in localStorage, falls back to prefers-color-scheme media query, toggles via useCallback, sets data-theme attribute on documentElement. Provider nesting: Theme > Language > Auth > AntApp. CSS uses [data-theme='dark'] and [data-theme='light'] selectors for color tokens. Ant Design ConfigProvider dynamically themed via useMemo with isDark. Kanit font applied globally. Sidebar nav uses translation keys (t.common.*). Language/theme selectors in header via Segmented and Switch components.
ai_warning: Known issues from AUDIT_REPORT.md: (1) translations/types.ts is dead code with competing Language type definitions; (2) :root CSS duplicates all dark values also in [data-theme='dark']; (3) Breadcrumbs in App.tsx ('Performance', 'Annual Dashboard', 'Settings', 'System') are hardcoded English -- bypass i18n; (4) index.html meta color-scheme hardcoded to 'dark' -- should be 'light dark' for proper light mode; (5) Sidebar.tsx has embedded <style> block breaking CSS architecture rules.

task: Auth service hardening: Env-var-based secrets with fail-fast on missing config, no hardcoded defaults
logic_changed: Removed all hardcoded fallback values for secrets. JWT_SECRET and PEPPER_SECRET are strictly required -- app calls log.Fatal() if missing (was 'default_jwt_secret'/'default_pepper_key'). ADMIN_USERNAMES is also strictly required. Password peppering: SHA256(password:username:pepperSecret) produces hex-encoded 64-char string, then bcrypt'd. This ensures same password across users produces different hashes (username in pepper mix), and breached bcrypt hashes are useless without the pepper secret. .env.example documents all required vars.
ai_warning: Peppering means changing PEPPER_SECRET or a username invalidates ALL existing passwords. The sha256+bcrypt chain is computationally expensive but intentional. GenerateToken still uses context.Background() -- cannot be cancelled/timeout via context. JWT expiry hardcoded to 7 days (time.Hour * 24 * 7) -- requires code redeploy to change. If ADMIN_USERNAMES is empty/missing, app refuses to start even if no admin users are needed.

task: Transaction list interface: Full table with inline editing, receipt preview, batch delete, responsive cards
logic_changed: TransactionList renders responsive table (desktop) or card grid (mobile, breakpoint 768px). Each row: type badge (INCOME/EXPENSE with color), category, description, amount, date, status (StatusBadge component), paid amount, receipt thumbnail. Inline editing: status dropdown (PENDING/PAID) triggers update(). Receipt images show as preview thumbnails. Batch selection: checkboxes for multi-select, batch delete via removeBatch(). Empty state shows illustration. Loading state via antd Spin. Desktop/mobile responsive with CSS media queries.
ai_warning: Status dropdown for INCOME transactions should be hidden/inactive since INCOME can't have status. Batch delete sends all IDs in single DELETE /api/transactions/batch -- large selections could hit URL/body size limits. Receipt images are Base64 strings in tags -- large receipts cause performance issues and DOM bloat. The StatusBadge component's color mapping should stay in sync with the ExpenseStatus type values.

task: UI polish: Responsive header, better chart UX, and dynamic Ant Design theme colors
logic_changed: Header: logo + app name left, controls (language, theme, date pickers) right. MonthPicker/YearPicker with arrow navigation. Sidebar CSS custom properties adapt to current theme. Charts: custom tooltips with Thai Baht amounts, gradient fills for area charts, legend optimized for mobile, abbreviated month tick formatting. Ant Design ConfigProvider computes tokens dynamically: colorPrimary #3b82f6, borderRadius 12, custom bg colors per mode. Background decorative blobs (CSS pseudo-elements with blur) for premium dark aesthetic.
ai_warning: Ant Design theme via useMemo with isDark -- all consuming components re-render on theme change. CSS .bg-blob elements use position:fixed + large blur filters that impact scroll performance on low-end devices. Chart tooltips hardcode Thai Baht symbol -- not wired to i18n. Sidebar active state relies on activePage prop string comparison -- if Page type values change, highlight silently breaks.

task: Paid cell UX fix: Receipt buttons in edit mode redesigned to prevent layout overflow in desktop table and mobile card
logic_changed: Desktop Paid cell (.edit-paid-cell): switched from flex-direction:column to flex-direction:row so InputNumber and receipt buttons share one horizontal row. When receipt IS attached: replaced 'OK' text badge with FileImageOutlined 'View' button (opens viewer modal) + DeleteOutlined danger button (removes receipt) — both type='text' size='small'. When NO receipt: replaced wide 'Receipt' label button with compact PlusOutlined icon button. Mobile card-edit-grid: for EXPENSE, receipt controls now live inline inside the Paid Amount row (.paid-amount-row flex container) instead of a separate full-width block below. For INCOME, receipt section kept as compact form-group with same icon-only buttons. Removed PictureOutlined import (no longer used). Removed CSS: .add-receipt-inline-btn, .receipt-status-inline, .receipt-ok. Added CSS: .receipt-actions-inline (flex row gap:2px), .paid-amount-row, .edit-paid-cell.income-edit-receipt.
ai_warning: The receipt-actions-inline pattern (View + Delete when attached, PlusOutlined Upload when empty) is duplicated 4 times in TransactionList.tsx (desktop EXPENSE, desktop INCOME, mobile EXPENSE, mobile INCOME). If receipt button behavior changes, update ALL four occurrences. The plus icon button uses type='text' which may be hard to discover for users expecting a visible upload button — monitor UX feedback. paid-amount-row uses flex:1 on InputNumber to fill available space alongside receipt buttons.

task: Project configuration and AI guidance: CLAUDE.md architecture docs, AI history protocol, audit report
logic_changed: CLAUDE.md documents complete architecture and dev workflow: layered backend, state-based frontend SPA, feature addition patterns (schema->models->repository->handler->router for backend; types->api->hook->component->App for frontend). Established AI_HISTORY.json protocol for compressed context tracking with targeted retrieval strategy (search by files_touched, not chronological scan). AUDIT_REPORT.md catalogued 10 code quality issues. BUG_FIXES.md documents 10 resolved bugs with fix descriptions.
ai_warning: CLAUDE.md is authoritative over .cursorrules and AI_DEVELOPMENT_GUIDE.md. writeJSON/writeError helpers in handlers/transaction.go are used by all handlers -- extract to shared helpers.go. Auth middleware returns plain text errors while handlers return JSON -- resolve this inconsistency. Always check AUDIT_REPORT.md before major refactoring for catalogued issues.

logic_changed: TransactionList mobile: Added .card-selection-overlay (absolute positioned checkbox overlay with custom ✓ pseudo-element) and .selection-mode-active class (left padding 52px to accommodate checkbox). MonthlyMixedChart: Added mobileOffset state for paginated 3-month window navigation. Older button increments offset (scrolls back), Newer decrements (toward latest). Data slice uses endIndex = lastDataIndex - mobileOffset. mobileOffset resets via useEffect when data or isMobile changes. Additional mobile tweaks: compact margins, abbreviated Y-axis labels (฿Xk), smaller tick fonts, dashed Income/Expense Change lines, YAxis width fixed for mobile.
ai_warning: mobileOffset useEffect depends on [data, isMobile] — removing either breaks reset-on-navigate behavior. The chart pagination slice logic (endIndex = lastDataIndex - offset) is order-sensitive — changing slice direction breaks Older/Newer semantics. .card-selection-overlay CSS checkbox uses appearance:none with custom :checked::after ✓ glyph — changing font or size may misalign the checkmark. The 4 receipt-action patterns from commit f57eacf (View+Delete when attached, PlusOutlined when empty) remain duplicated across desktop/mobile × EXPENSE/INCOME — changes to receipt UX must update ALL four.
task: Mobile UX: Selection mode checkboxes + MonthlyMixedChart pagination with Older/Newer buttons
Group Collaboration feature: multi-user groups with roles (OWNER/EDITOR/VIEWER), invites, activity logging, and author tracking
logic_changed: Added role column to group_members (OWNER/EDITOR/VIEWER, default EDITOR for new joins, first member auto-promoted to OWNER via MIN(rowid)). Activity log table gated by GroupHasMultipleMembers check — no overhead for single-member/personal groups. Invite codes are UUID, single-use, 24h expiry, auto-cleaned on validation. JWT carries groupRole as snapshot at login time — users must re-login after join/leave/role-change. createdByUsername populated via SQL CASE WHEN gm.cnt > 1 subquery (CROSS JOIN counting group members) — only visible in multi-member groups. GroupRoleMiddleware applied via chi r.With() to individual write routes (EDITOR+), read routes remain open to all members. Frontend Dashboard polls every 30s when memberCount > 1 for real-time sync. GroupPage has invite generation (copy-to-clipboard), join form, leave with confirmation modal (sole-owner guard), member table with role-colored tags, and activity feed.
ai_warning: groupRole in JWT is a snapshot — any role change or join/leave requires re-login. Activity logging silently fails (log warning only) to avoid breaking user operations. The CROSS JOIN in transaction queries binds groupID as first param — reordering params breaks createdByUsername. LeaveGroup handler checks sole OWNER guard BEFORE logging activity. RemoveMember INSERT restores user to personal group as OWNER — changing this role breaks the ownership model. GroupPage join also triggers page reload after 1.5s delay. The .tx-author-badge CSS uses fixed 18px circle — longer usernames will be truncated to first character only.

task: Phase 1-2: Multi-group/wallet support — users can create/switch/manage multiple groups, each with isolated transactions like separate wallets. JWT-based group switching, group selector in sidebar, cross-group transaction copy, annual dashboard group awareness.
logic_changed: Changed core invariant from 1-user-1-group to N-user-N-group: (1) AddMember() removed DELETE existing memberships before INSERT — users can belong to multiple groups. (2) Added CreateUserGroup(repo), DeleteMember(repo), DeleteGroup(repo-owner-only), ListUserGroups(repo) for group lifecycle. (3) AuthService.GenerateTokenForGroup(groupID) generates JWT embedding specific groupId + groupRole — Login defaults to personal group (first created = '{username}\''s Group'), SwitchGroup re-issues JWT for target group. (4) New endpoints: GET/POST /api/me/groups, DELETE /api/me/groups/{id}, POST /api/me/switch-group. (5) JoinGroup now returns fresh JWT instead of 'please re-login'. LeaveGroup auto-switches to first remaining group (via DeleteMember which only removes membership, no personal-group restoration) and returns new JWT. (6) POST /api/transactions/batch-to-group for cross-group copy with targetGroupId + membership validation. (7) Frontend AuthContext tracks myGroups[] + activeGroup, switchGroup() updates localStorage token + triggers refresh on activeGroup.id change. Sidebar has Ant Select dropdown for group switching + inline 'Create New Wallet' with keyboard input. CopyTransactionsModal shows target group selector when myGroups.length > 1.
ai_warning: DeleteMember vs RemoveMember: DeleteMember only DELETEs the membership row (used by LeaveGroup when user keeps other groups). RemoveMember still exists for admin panel — it restores user to personal group. Do NOT confuse the two. GenerateTokenForGroup with empty groupID defaults to GetUserGroupID which NOW uses ORDER BY g.created_at ASC (personal group first). The refresh-on-activeGroup-id effect in App.tsx fires on every group switch — if useTransactions dependencies change, this could double-fire. Backend Go files use CRLF line endings — Edit tool may fail matching strings containing special characters; use shortest unique substring for edits.

task: Phase 3: Loan/Debt Tracker — new feature for tracking borrow/lend with sub-ledger entries (withdrawal/deposit/installment), computed summaries, receipt attachments, and installment date calculation
logic_changed: Added loans + loan_entries tables with FK CASCADE. Loan types: BORROW (we borrow) / LEND (we lend). Entry types: WITHDRAWAL (spend from loan pool, BORROW only), DEPOSIT (return to pool / debtor pays back), INSTALLMENT (scheduled payment). buildLoanDetail() computes totalWithdrawn/Deposited/Installments + outstanding (BORROW: principal - installments; LEND: principal - deposits) + progressPercent. CalculateNextInstallmentDate() generates all due dates from startDate + paymentDay + termMonths, flags overdue. Frontend LoanTracker: 2-panel layout — left: loan list table (type badge, counterparty, principal, outstanding, progress bar, close/delete actions); right: selected loan detail with summary cards (Principal/Paid/Outstanding/Progress/Available Pool) + entry table (type badge, amount, date, receipt thumbnail, delete). Create loan modal with type toggle (BORROW/LEND), term/installment/paymentDay fields. Add entry modal with type dropdown (context-sensitive: BORROW shows WITHDRAWAL/DEPOSIT/INSTALLMENT; LEND shows only DEPOSIT), receipt upload with Canvas compression (max 800px, JPEG 0.7). All routes under /api/loans/* with GroupRoleMiddleware(EDITOR+) on write endpoints.
ai_warning: Loan routes initially nested under /api/me/loans (sed insert error) — FIXED: moved to /api/loans level (parallel to transactions/categories). Verify after any router.go edit. The loan entries FK uses ON DELETE CASCADE. buildLoanDetail now uses batch ListEntriesByLoanIDs + pure computeLoanDetail function — avoid reverting to per-loan N+1 pattern. SwitchGroup/JoinGroup/LeaveGroup MUST look up full user via userRepo.GetByID() before calling GenerateTokenForGroup — partial User struct causes 'invalid token claims' (Bug 1 fixed during testing). GroupHandler now requires userRepo in constructor (NewGroupHandler signature changed).

task: Post-test bug fixes + polish: AuthContext dependency cycle, hardcoded labels, N+1 query optimization, loan route relocation, missing return fix
logic_changed: Bug 1 (Critical): GenerateTokenForGroup called with &models.User{ID: userID} which had empty Username/Role → JWT claims failed. Fixed by adding userRepo to GroupHandler and doing full user lookup via GetByID() before token generation in SwitchGroup/JoinGroup/LeaveGroup. Also populated GroupName from groupInfo.Name instead of hardcoded "". Bug 2: Loan routes nested under /api/me/loans (sed put them inside /me block). Fixed by extracting to /api/loans level parallel to transactions/categories. Other fixes: AuthContext dependency cycle (useRef breaks refreshMyGroups↔switchGroupFn), hardcoded 'Loans' label (added t.common.loans EN/TH translations), buildLoanDetail N+1 (added ListEntriesByLoanIDs batch query + pure computeLoanDetail), CreateLoan missing return after counterparty check. All 9 tests passed: multi-group CRUD, transaction isolation, cross-group copy, leave guard, BORROW/LEND loan math (outstanding/progress all correct), loan close, annual per-group, group delete.
ai_warning: Router.go indentation is critical — loan routes must be at 3-tab level (inside /api block, NOT inside /me). After any router edit, grep for 'r.Route("/loans"' and verify it's 3 tabs deep. GroupHandler now takes userRepo as 4th param BEFORE authService — main.go line 63 must match. The test report (TEST_REPORT.md) shows actual results from live API testing — if re-running tests, update BASE URL and token flow.

task: UI polish: LoanTracker mobile-responsive redesign, dark mode color fix, entry sorting + inline editing, antd deprecation cleanup across Sidebar/LoanTracker
logic_changed: (1) Mobile LoanTracker: Full rewrite with card-based layout on <=768px. Desktop: 2-column grid (loan list + detail panel). Mobile: single-column, loan list as cards (name, counterparty, type/status badges, principal/owed/progress stats, close/delete), tap card → full-screen detail with Back button. Summary stats use smaller fontSize (16 vs 18) on mobile. Entry list: desktop table → mobile cards (type badge, amount, date, description, receipt thumbnail, edit/delete buttons). Modals: width 95vw, top:10 on mobile. (2) Dark mode CSS fix: replaced non-existent vars (--bg-elevated→--bg-secondary, --border-color→--border-subtle, --blue-900→--blue-glow) with project's actual CSS variables from index.css — text no longer blends into background. (3) Entries sort: newest-first via [...entries].sort((a,b)=>b.date.localeCompare(a.date)||b.createdAt.localeCompare(a.createdAt)). (4) Entry editing: delete+create pattern (backend has no PATCH for entries). Edit button on every entry (table row + mobile card), opens same modal with pre-filled values, title switches to 'Edit Entry', okText to 'Save'. (5) Sidebar: dropdownRender→popupRender. (6) LoanTracker: valueStyle→styles.content on all 7 Statistic instances, message→App.useApp() hook for dynamic theme. (7) GroupPage: Leave modal width 95vw maxWidth:420, Member table scroll x. (8) CopyTransactionsModal: table scroll y:280 for vertical constraint.
ai_warning: Entry editing uses delete-then-create pattern — if create fails after delete succeeds, the original entry is lost. To mitigate: backend could add PATCH /api/loans/{id}/entries/{eid} in future. The sorted entries clone uses spread operator — mutating loan.entries directly would break React's immutability contract. Canvas compression is inline now (not shared with TransactionForm's compressImage) — changes to compression logic need updating in both places. Statistic styles.content nesting: styles={{ content: { fontSize: 16 } }} — the double }} correctly closes both content object and styles object.

task: LoanTracker build recovery, close confirmation safety, and expanded TH/EN i18n coverage across core UI
logic_changed: Recovered LoanTracker.tsx from malformed JSX/duplicated loanColumns by rebuilding the component with complete desktop/mobile loan list, detail panel, create loan modal, add/edit entry modal, and receipt compression. Wrapped loan Close actions in Popconfirm on both desktop table and mobile cards to prevent accidental status changes. Expanded translations/index.ts from minimal common/dashboard keys into a broad TH/EN dictionary covering common labels, transactions, copy modal, categories, group, loans, auth, annual dashboard, and theme text. App.tsx now passes Ant Design locale (th_TH/en_US) into ConfigProvider and translates breadcrumbs/theme tooltip. Core components now read labels/messages/dropdowns from useLanguage() instead of hardcoded English. StatusBadge now localizes PAID/PENDING. Chart labels and empty states were partially localized and Baht mojibake was replaced with the real Baht symbol/escaped output where needed. npm run build passes after fixes.
ai_warning: Several components still contain domain enum values (INCOME/EXPENSE/BORROW/LEND/ACTIVE) for API/type logic; only display labels should be localized. TransactionList uses const { t: tr } = useLanguage() because map callbacks commonly name each transaction t; do not rename back to t without also renaming callback variables. Loan entry editing still uses delete-then-create, so a failed create after delete can lose the original entry. CategoryDonutChart/MonthlyMixedChart were touched for encoding cleanup; if UTF-8 errors reappear, rewrite affected files as UTF-8 and prefer ASCII/Unicode escapes for Thai text inside code.

task: Fix translation type error (pool), reorganize sidebar categories, and resolve dark mode text blending in Loan Tracker
logic_changed: 1) Added 'pool' key to TranslationKeys interface and TH/EN dictionaries to resolve TypeScript error in LoanTracker t.loansPage.pool. 2) Reorganized Sidebar structure by replacing the collapsible Settings sub-menu with clear, text-labeled flat sections (Finance: Dashboard, Annual, Loans; Settings: Categories, My Group, Admin Panel) using the sidebar-section-title class. 3) Resolved dark mode text blending issue on the Loan/Loan Detail pages by adding '!important' to the body text color rule in index.css and applying highly specific class selectors (.loan-tracker-container, .loan-card, .entry-card, .loan-detail-card) in App.css mapped with CSS variables (--text-primary and --text-secondary) to override Ant Design's default text colors.
ai_warning: The Sidebar reorganization flattened the navigation hierarchy; do not revert to a nested/collapsible Settings sub-menu without updating both styles and tests. The Loan Tracker text color fixes rely on specific className selectors with !important flags in App.css to override Ant Design's CSS-in-JS defaults. Ensure any newly added cards or text blocks in LoanTracker.tsx use these classNames to avoid text blending issues in dark mode.

task: Wallet/Group: Separate owner (user_id) from recorder (created_by) — manual owner selection, per-member wallet balance view, transaction owner column, polling reduction
logic_changed: Phase 0 (Owner vs Recorder separation): Added created_by TEXT column to transactions table with ALTER TABLE migration + backfill (created_by = user_id WHERE created_by IS NULL). Transactions now have: user_id = wallet owner (manually selectable, defaults to JWT user), created_by = recorder (always JWT user, cant change). CreateTransactionRequest gets optional userId field; handler validates requested owner is group member via GetMemberRole(). Create/CreateBatch repo methods changed signature: ownerUserID + createdByID instead of single userID. UpdateTransactionRequest supports changing userId (owner). GetByMonthYear/GetByID queries now JOIN users rec_u ON t.created_by and users own_u ON t.user_id, returning createdByUsername (recorder) and ownerUsername (wallet owner) separately. Phase 1 (Wallet Summary): GET /api/transactions/wallet-summary?month=&year= returns per-member breakdown grouped by user_id (wallet owner), using LEFT JOIN so members with no transactions show zeroes. Phase 3 (WalletView UI): New WalletView.tsx with inline styles (not dependent on App.css since it kept getting truncated), compact 3-column horizontal card layout per member (Income | Expense | Balance), single-line group total bar (income - expense = balance). Dashboard.tsx gets Segmented toggle 'Dashboard/Wallet' visible only for multi-member groups. TransactionForm.tsx gets member <Select> dropdown for manual owner selection (visible when memberCount>1, default=current user). TransactionList.tsx: Desktop table gets 'Owner' column showing ownerUsername; mobile cards get card-owner text line. AuthContext polling reduced from 30s to 3min (3*60000). SQL CASE WHEN gm.cnt>1 gates removed from GetByMonthYear/GetByID — ownerUsername and createdByUsername now always returned regardless of group size. CROSS JOIN gm subquery removed from these queries.
ai_warning: CRLF line endings in Go files cause Edit tool to silently fail — always use Bash+Python for Go file edits. App.css modifications are unreliable (file gets truncated/overwritten by linter) — prefer inline styles for critical new components. The Create repo now takes (ownerUserID, createdByID, groupID) — handlers must pass JWT userID as createdByID (3rd param), NOT as ownerUserID (2nd param defaults to JWT user unless req.UserID overrides). schema.go was truncated to 364 lines during edits and needed rebuilding — after any schema.go edit, verify file ends with seedCategories function closing braces and return nil. The transaction list hasOwnerColumn check uses transactions.some(t => t.ownerUsername) to conditionally show the Owner column. WalletView.tsx uses all inline styles — do NOT add wallet CSS to App.css unless also updating the component to use className instead. Batch-to-group handler still passes userID for both ownerUserID and createdByID (self-assignment for cross-group copy). Translations: transactions.owner key added (EN: 'Owner', TH: 'เจ้าของ'); transactions.createdBy now means wallet owner badge.

task: Fix transaction owner column binding/layout and enlarge wallet menu typography
logic_changed: TransactionList desktop rows now render a real owner <td> when the Owner column is enabled, so category/description/type cells no longer shift under the wrong headers. Owner names are shown as text in the Owner column, while recorder info stays as secondary metadata only when createdByUsername differs from ownerUsername. Mobile cards now show the owner name line with optional recorder text. Dashboard wallet toggle styling was enlarged and made full-width on mobile to reduce cramped labels. Sidebar wallet/group selector and section heading typography were increased for readability.
ai_warning: TransactionList still conditionally shows the Owner column via transactions.some(t => t.ownerUsername); if backend data regresses to null ownerUsername, the column disappears again. Recorder text is intentionally secondary metadata, not a primary column; keep row cell counts aligned with the header whenever this table changes. Sidebar typography is currently adjusted via the component's embedded <style> block, so matching changes may need edits in both Sidebar.tsx and App.css if the sidebar styling is later centralized.

task: Restyle dashboard wallet toggle to match language selector and improve wallet card readability/spacing
logic_changed: Dashboard view toggle now reuses the language-selector Segmented styling via a shared class, with responsive full-width behavior on mobile. WalletView was rebuilt from compact inline 3-column text rows into class-based cards with a clearer hierarchy: member header, income/expense stat panels, and a dedicated balance row. Group total is now rendered as a separate summary card with larger values and explicit balance labeling to reduce visual overlap with member cards.
ai_warning: This pass intentionally overrides earlier wallet CSS later in App.css instead of deleting the older block, because App.css is large and historically brittle during edits. If wallet styles are refactored later, remove or consolidate the duplicated wallet rules carefully so the later readability override block remains the winner. Build verification could not be rerun in the current shell because the available Node runtime was v10.24.1, which is too old for TypeScript 5/Vite 8 syntax.

task: Soften dashboard wallet segmented control UI
logic_changed: Dashboard wallet toggle styling was refined from a hard rectangular segmented button into a softer glassy control: added translucent container, subtle border/shadow, rounded thumb, calmer inactive text color, stronger active gradient, and slightly taller mobile hit area. The goal was to make the control feel integrated with the dashboard surface instead of looking like a detached action button.
ai_warning: The dashboard toggle still shares the base `language-selector` class with TH/EN and then layers `dashboard-view-segmented` overrides on top. If the shared Segmented styling changes later, re-check both controls together so one does not inherit awkward colors, padding, or thumb shapes from the other.

task: Remove blue rectangular active artifact from dashboard wallet toggle
logic_changed: dashboard-view-segmented now explicitly clears `.ant-segmented-item-selected` background and item box-shadow so the control only shows the rounded thumb, not the inherited rectangular active fill from `language-selector`.
ai_warning: The dashboard toggle intentionally reuses `language-selector` and must override its selected-state background. If the base TH/EN segmented styles change, verify the dashboard toggle still removes the inherited active rectangle.

task: Decouple dashboard wallet toggle styling from TH/EN active-state logic
logic_changed: Dashboard toggle no longer shares the `language-selector` class directly. It now uses only `dashboard-view-segmented`, while App.css gives it a visually similar segmented look with its own transparent item background and dedicated thumb styling. This preserves the blue selected pill without inheriting the rectangular active fill from the language switcher.
ai_warning: Do not re-add `language-selector` to the dashboard toggle unless you also want to inherit TH/EN selected-state behavior again. Keep visual similarity by copying style tokens, not by sharing the exact class when the UX requirements diverge.

task: Force dashboard wallet selected state to use blue thumb without white overlay
logic_changed: dashboard-view-segmented now explicitly sets the selected item background to transparent, removes selected box-shadow, and forces the selected label color to white. This ensures Ant Design's default selected panel does not render as a white rectangle over the custom blue thumb.
ai_warning: If Ant Design Segmented internals change, re-check both `.ant-segmented-item-selected` and `.ant-segmented-item-label` inside dashboard-view-segmented. The visual relies on the thumb being the only active background layer.

task: Stop relying on Ant Segmented thumb for dashboard wallet toggle active state
logic_changed: Dashboard toggle active styling now uses `.ant-segmented-item-selected` itself as the blue rounded pill and hides `.ant-segmented-thumb` entirely. This avoids inconsistent layering where Ant's selected panel/thumb produced white backgrounds or invisible text in the custom dashboard control.
ai_warning: For this control, selected-state visuals are item-driven, not thumb-driven. If someone later re-enables `.ant-segmented-thumb`, they must remove the selected item gradient or the layers will fight again.

task: Refine dashboard wallet toggle to a more minimal pill style with centered labels
logic_changed: Dashboard toggle container and selected item were softened into fully rounded pill shapes with lighter borders/shadows and a calmer blue active fill. The segmented item label now uses flex centering with fixed min-height so text stays visually centered inside each pill on both desktop and mobile.
ai_warning: Label centering for the dashboard toggle depends on `.ant-segmented-item-label` being turned into an inline-flex container. If Ant Design changes that internal markup, re-check vertical centering before tweaking padding or line-height.

task: Replace dashboard wallet Ant Segmented with custom toggle to stop disappearing active highlight
logic_changed: Dashboard/Wallet view switch no longer uses Ant Design Segmented internals. It now renders two plain buttons bound directly to `viewMode`, with `.dashboard-view-option.active` controlling the blue selected pill. This avoids unstable active-state rendering where Ant's internal selected/thumb layers caused the blue highlight to disappear after interaction or rerender.
ai_warning: The dashboard toggle is now a custom control, not an Ant component. Future visual changes should target `.dashboard-view-segmented`, `.dashboard-view-option`, and `.dashboard-view-option.active` instead of Ant selectors like `.ant-segmented-item-selected`.

task: Widen desktop shell so dashboard uses more horizontal space on Windows
logic_changed: Increased the desktop max-width of both `.header-content` and `.app-main` from 1400px to 1680px so the top dashboard region can stretch wider and feel proportionate to the lower content area on large Windows screens.
ai_warning: This change widens the whole authenticated shell on desktop, not just the dashboard. If future layouts start to feel too loose on extra-wide screens, consider introducing view-specific max-widths instead of shrinking `app-main` globally again.

task: Fix add-transaction owner selection not being sent in create payload
logic_changed: TransactionForm was storing the selected owner in local/form state but did not include `userId` when building the create request. Both INCOME and EXPENSE submissions now pass `userId` from the selected owner override, so backend owner assignment can differ from the logged-in recorder as intended. The owner dropdown label/placeholder was also switched from `createdBy` to `owner` for clarity.
ai_warning: If owner selection breaks again, inspect request construction in TransactionForm before debugging backend handlers. The backend already honors `req.UserID`; this bug was caused by the frontend omitting `userId` from the POST payload.

task: Transaction owner editing, mobile group activity collapse, annual chart currency cleanup, and wallet summary paid-overage reconciliation
logic_changed: TransactionList edit mode now supports changing the transaction owner (`userId`) for multi-member groups on both desktop and mobile, while save/cancel actions use clearer pill-style buttons. Dashboard recent group activity is collapsible on mobile and defaults to collapsed. MonthlyMixedChart replaced mojibake currency output with stable THB formatters for the left axis and tooltip. WalletView group total balance now mirrors dashboard net-balance logic (`totalIncome - totalPaid - totalPending`). Backend wallet summary aggregation was corrected so paid expenses use `paid_amount` when present, and wallet `totalExpense` now reflects actual paid overages plus pending amounts; this keeps per-member and group wallet totals aligned with dashboard metrics when paid amounts exceed the original expense amount.
ai_warning: Wallet summary math now distinguishes three concepts: `totalExpense` = paid_amount for PAID rows plus amount for PENDING rows, `totalPaid` = paid_amount for PAID rows only, and wallet group net balance is intentionally calculated from `totalIncome - totalPaid - totalPending` to match the dashboard summary cards. If wallet cards or API consumers assume `totalExpense == totalPaid + totalPending` from the original transaction amount, re-check them after any future repository changes. TransactionList owner editing depends on `groupInfo.members` from AuthContext and will only render the owner selector for multi-member groups.

task: Month-over-month change indicator in TransactionList — shows percentage increase/decrease for each transaction vs previous month
logic_changed: Added MomChange/MomChangeMap types to transaction.ts for tracking previousAmount, currentAmount, percentChange, isNew, isUnchanged, prevTxId. useTransactions hook now fetches previous month transactions in parallel (Promise.all) and computes a momChanges map via useMemo. Matching is done by composite key: userId|type|category|description (same fields the clone/copy feature preserves). Percent change is computed as ((curr - prev) / prev) * 100 rounded to 1 decimal place. In TransactionList: desktop table gets a new '∆ MoM' column header + col-mom cells between Amount and Status; mobile cards show the MoM badge between card-amount and type-badge. Color coding: green for 'good' changes (expense decrease or income increase), red for 'bad' changes (expense increase or income decrease), arrow indicators (↑/↓), NEW label for items without a previous month match, '—' for unchanged. Tooltip shows previous/current amounts and percentage. CSS uses existing theme variables (--green-glow, --red-glow, --green-500, --red-400, --blue-400, --blue-glow) for theme-aware coloring. Translations added: common.momChange (table header), transactions.momNew, transactions.momUnchanged, transactions.momTooltip with TH/EN versions.
ai_warning: No backend changes were made — all MoM computation happens on the frontend via dual fetch (current + previous month). The makeMomKey function uses userId|type|category|description — if the clone/copy feature changes what fields it preserves, update this key. Previous month lookup keeps only the first duplicate match by key (if multiple transactions have same composite key in prev month, only the first one is matched). Transactions with userId === null use '__nobody__' as fallback in the composite key. The renderMomBadge helper takes a momLabels object, not the full translation context — if new badges are needed, extend the interface. CSS mom-badge class uses hardcoded text-transform:uppercase on mom-new only — non-blue badges inherit text formatting from their content. The card-mom CSS uses align-self: flex-end to position inside the column-layout card-amount-box.

task: Admin password reset: admin can reset any user's password to a new value
logic_changed: Backend: Added UpdatePassword(id, passwordHash) to UserRepository. Exposed HashPassword(password, username) on AuthService (public wrapper around the private hashWithPepper + bcrypt chain) so AdminHandler can hash passwords with the same scheme. AdminHandler now takes authService as constructor param (NewAdminHandler signature changed). ResetUserPassword handler at PATCH /api/admin/users/{id}/reset-password: validates password >=6 chars, fetches user for username-based peppering, hashes, calls UpdatePassword. Returns { message, username } on success. Route added inside /api/admin group (behind AuthMiddleware + AdminOnly). main.go updated to pass authService to NewAdminHandler. Fixed Go build error: removed unused pepperedPassword variable left over from Register refactor. Frontend: AdminPanel user table gets 'Actions' column with LockOutlined 'Reset Password' button. Modal for password input (minLength=6), Enter key submits. CRITICAL FIX: AdminPanel now calls hashPassword(resetTargetUser.username, newPassword) before sending — this produces SHA256(password:username_lowercase) matching the client-side hashing used by Login/Register forms. Without this, the backend stores SHA256(plaintext:username:pepper) but login sends SHA256(SHA256_hash:username:pepper), causing mismatch and login failure.
ai_warning: FRONTEND PASSWORD FLOW: Login.tsx and Register.tsx both call hashPassword(username, password) from api/security.ts which produces SHA256(password:username_lowercase) via SubtleCrypto. The backend then peppers this hash with SHA256(hash:username:pepper). When building any admin feature that touches passwords, ALWAYS hash on the client side via hashPassword() before sending to /api/admin/users/{id}/reset-password. The admin panel labels are in a local 'l' object (not the main i18n system) — if the app is fully migrated to useLanguage(), move these labels to translations/index.ts. Router.go uses CRLF line endings and tab indentation — Python script needed for edits; the Edit tool cannot match tab-indented Go code reliably. The password peppering scheme (SHA256(password:username:pepperSecret) → bcrypt) means the username is part of the hash input — the handler fetches the user by ID first to ensure the right username is used.
